content:
# IVX END-TO-END FINAL CONFIRMATION FROM RORK

MODE: AUDIT + VERIFY + REPORT
PRIORITY: P0
GOAL: Provide one complete, honest, item-by-item confirmation of all IVX work completed yesterday and today.

DO NOT:
- give a summary only
- say “everything is done” without proof
- combine different projects
- mix Expo and Kotlin
- mark anything LIVE without production evidence
- mark anything VERIFIED without test evidence
- hide pending work
- report local code as production
- report plan items as implemented

==================================================
STATUS DEFINITIONS
==================================================

Use only these statuses:

- LIVE PRODUCTION
- DEPLOYED BUT NOT VERIFIED
- CODE COMPLETE, NOT DEPLOYED
- PARTIALLY IMPLEMENTED
- PLAN ONLY
- BLOCKED
- NOT FOUND
- PRESERVED FOR PHASE 2
- REMOVED
- FAILED

For each item provide:
- current status
- exact file(s)
- GitHub commit SHA
- deployment ID
- production route or screen
- test result
- proof
- remaining work

==================================================
SECTION 1 — PROJECT SEPARATION
==================================================

Confirm:

1. Expo/React Native app
2. Backend
3. Landing page
4. Kotlin project
5. GitHub repository
6. Production branch
7. Render service
8. Supabase project
9. Expo/EAS project
10. Android package

Required:

- `expo/` = active Phase 1 app
- `backend/` = active backend
- `android-ivx-holdings/` = preserved for Phase 2
- Kotlin not used in current Expo build
- Kotlin not deleted
- Kotlin not mixed into Expo
- no second production app

==================================================
SECTION 2 — EXPO APP
==================================================

Confirm status for:

1. Home
2. Invest
3. Market
4. Portfolio
5. Chat
6. Profile
7. CRM
8. Owner Login
9. Owner AI
10. Live Work
11. Members
12. Investors
13. Buyers
14. Properties
15. Deals
16. Reels
17. Deal Detail
18. View Deal
19. Invest Now
20. Notifications
21. Image upload
22. PDF upload
23. Video upload
24. Search
25. Settings
26. Admin/Owner controls

For every module return:
- route
- component
- API used
- production status
- test result
- screenshot or live proof
- remaining issue

==================================================
SECTION 3 — HOME FEED DESIGN
==================================================

Confirm whether production currently shows:

1. Regular Investment Card
2. Regular Investment Card
3. Regular Investment Card
4. Casa Rosario Reel Card

Verify regular cards:
- compact card
- up to 8 pictures
- horizontal swipe
- image counter
- View Deal
- Invest Now
- like/comment/save/share

Verify reel:
- reel design only
- self-adjusting height
- no clipping
- no overlap
- no infinite loading

Return:
- Home component file
- regular card component file
- reel component file
- production feed payload
- `display_type` values
- live screenshot

==================================================
SECTION 4 — LANDING PAGE
==================================================

Confirm:

- landing page is live
- correct feed design is live
- registration works
- waitlist works
- deals load
- reels load
- View Deal works
- Invest Now works
- no jumping
- no stale content
- no mock data
- production API is used

Return:
- URL
- deploy ID
- deployed SHA
- test results
- screenshots

==================================================
SECTION 5 — IVX IA SENIOR DEVELOPER
==================================================

Confirm each capability independently:

1. Owner authentication
2. Developer intent classification
3. Senior developer runtime
4. Tool registry
5. Variable resolver
6. GitHub access
7. Repository inspection
8. File editing
9. Command runner
10. Lint
11. Typecheck
12. Unit tests
13. Integration tests
14. Commit creation
15. GitHub push
16. Render deployment
17. Production verification
18. Live Work
19. Trace IDs
20. Failure handling
21. Cancel
22. Retry
23. Timeout
24. Rollback
25. Structured proof response

For each return:
- file/function
- last real execution date
- last task ID
- last trace ID
- GitHub SHA
- deploy ID
- PASS/FAIL
- live proof

Do not confirm Senior Developer as complete unless the actual IVX Owner AI executed a real task end to end.

==================================================
SECTION 6 — AI PROVIDER
==================================================

Confirm:

- active provider
- model
- base URL family
- variable name
- key loaded
- key valid
- provider ready
- HTTP 200
- fallback status
- Rork dependency status

Verify:
- short message
- long message
- image
- PDF
- developer task
- persistence
- no 401
- no 402
- no invalid endpoint
- no retry loop

==================================================
SECTION 7 — AUTHENTICATION
==================================================

Confirm:

- owner login
- owner logout
- session persistence
- invalid password
- expired session
- refresh token
- recovery
- owner-only routes
- non-owner denial
- admin access
- member access
- investor access
- buyer access

Return exact HTTP statuses and trace IDs.

==================================================
SECTION 8 — DATA
==================================================

Confirm physical database, API, and UI counts for:

- Members
- Investors
- Buyers
- Leads
- Prospects
- Deals
- Project videos
- Messages
- Notifications

Specifically explain:

- why Members show 4 instead of expected 36
- why Buyers show 0 or 1
- why Investors show 875 or 1161
- why Project Videos showed 8 vs 17

Do not modify or delete data.

Return:
- database count
- API count
- UI count
- variance
- root cause
- recovery status

==================================================
SECTION 9 — THIRD-PARTY CONNECTIONS
==================================================

Confirm:

- GitHub
- Render
- Supabase Auth
- Supabase Database
- Supabase Realtime
- Supabase Storage
- Vercel AI Gateway or direct OpenAI
- Expo/EAS
- FCM
- APNs
- Expo Push
- Redis
- CDN
- DNS
- TLS
- email
- SMS
- AWS if used

For each return:
- enabled
- configured
- live
- tested
- HTTP/result status
- last successful test
- remaining issue

==================================================
SECTION 10 — AUTONOMOUS
==================================================

Confirm status for:

- autonomous worker
- scheduler
- queue
- live dashboard
- organic lead acquisition
- purchasing-intent notifications
- daily 24-hour report
- agent dashboard
- worker heartbeat
- job history
- retries
- pause/resume
- notifications

Classify each as:
- LIVE PRODUCTION
- PARTIAL
- PLAN ONLY
- NOT FOUND

Return real job IDs and last-run timestamps.

==================================================
SECTION 11 — PERFORMANCE
==================================================

Confirm:

- Redis active or inactive
- autoscaling active or inactive
- Render plan
- replica count
- database indexes applied
- connection pooling
- cursor pagination
- response compression
- APM
- alerting
- metrics
- load-test results

Return:
- 10 users
- 50 users
- 100 users
- 500 users
- p95
- p99
- RPS
- error rate
- breaking point

State clearly:
100M READINESS = VERIFIED / PARTIAL / NOT VERIFIED

==================================================
SECTION 12 — BUILD AND APK
==================================================

Confirm:

- Expo project ID
- Android package
- app version
- versionCode
- production profile
- signed APK
- APK filename
- APK SHA-256
- APK file size
- GitHub source SHA
- direct download URL
- HTTP 200
- build ID
- build timestamp

Confirm:
- APK built from `expo/`
- Kotlin not included
- complete app included
- no debug menu
- no test credentials
- no localhost
- no Rork runtime dependency

==================================================
SECTION 13 — TEST RESULTS
==================================================

Return exact totals for:

- TypeScript
- Lint
- Unit tests
- Integration tests
- Backend tests
- Expo checks
- Navigation tests
- Feed tests
- AI tests
- Auth tests
- Attachment tests
- RLS tests
- Security tests
- Build tests

Do not report “all pass” without totals.

==================================================
SECTION 14 — DEPLOYMENT PROOF
==================================================

Return:

- repository
- branch
- GitHub SHA
- remote SHA
- Render deploy ID
- Render deployed SHA
- landing deploy ID
- Expo update ID
- APK build ID
- runtime health SHA
- boot time
- health status
- readiness status

All SHAs must match where applicable.

==================================================
SECTION 15 — COMPLETE WORK MAP
==================================================

Create a master table:

| Work Item | Yesterday/Today | File(s) | Status | Git SHA | Deploy ID | Live Route | Tests | Remaining Work |

Include every item worked on yesterday and today.

Do not omit failed, reverted, abandoned, partial, or plan-only work.

==================================================
MANDATORY FINAL SUMMARY
==================================================

TOTAL ITEMS:
LIVE PRODUCTION:
DEPLOYED NOT VERIFIED:
CODE COMPLETE NOT DEPLOYED:
PARTIAL:
PLAN ONLY:
BLOCKED:
FAILED:
PRESERVED FOR PHASE 2:

PHASE 1 EXPO:
- Status:

IVX IA SENIOR DEVELOPER:
- Status:

AUTONOMOUS:
- Status:

DATA RECOVERY:
- Status:

PERFORMANCE:
- Status:

APK:
- Status:

KOTLIN PHASE 2:
- Status:

TOP REMAINING BLOCKERS:
1.
2.
3.
4.
5.

FINAL STATUS:
VERIFIED / PARTIAL / FAILED / BLOCKED

FINAL RULE:
Do not say “all work is live” unless every item has a real Git SHA, deploy ID, production route, test result, and live proof.
